code:
local world
local body
local shape
local fixture

function love.load()
    love.physics.setMeter(64)
    world = love.physics.newWorld(0, 9.81 * 64, true)

    body = love.physics.newBody(world, 200, 120, "dynamic")

title: State Machine
description: A minimal state machine for managing game scenes (Menu, Game, Pause, etc.). Save as StateMachine.lua.
code:
-- StateMachine.lua
-- Usage:
--   local SM = require("StateMachine")
--   local states = SM.new({ menu = MenuState.new(), game = GameState.new() })
--   states:change("menu")
--   -- in love.update(dt): states:update(dt)
--   -- in love.draw():    states:draw()

local StateMachine = {}
StateMachine.__index = StateMachine

function StateMachine.new(states)
    return setmetatable({
        states  = states or {},
        current = nil,
    }, StateMachine)
end

-- Switch to a named state. Calls :enter(...) on the new state if it exists.
function StateMachine:change(name, ...)
    if self.current and self.current.exit then
        self.current:exit()
    end
    self.current = self.states[name]
    if self.current and self.current.enter then
        self.current:enter(...)
    end
end

function StateMachine:update(dt)
    if self.current and self.current.update then
        self.current:update(dt)
    end
end

function StateMachine:draw()
    if self.current and self.current.draw then
        self.current:draw()
    end
end

function StateMachine:keypressed(key)
    if self.current and self.current.keypressed then
        self.current:keypressed(key)
    end
end

return StateMachine

title: Scene Bootstrap (main.lua)
description: Wires StateMachine + multiple scene states in main.lua. Assumes StateMachine.lua and states/MenuState.lua, states/GameState.lua etc. are present.
code:
-- main.lua - scene bootstrap
local StateMachine = require("StateMachine")
local MenuState    = require("states/MenuState")
local GameState    = require("states/GameState")
local PauseState   = require("states/PauseState")
local GameOverState = require("states/GameOverState")

local gameStates

function love.load()
    gameStates = StateMachine.new({
        menu     = MenuState.new(),
        game     = GameState.new(),
        pause    = PauseState.new(),
        gameover = GameOverState.new(),
    })
    gameStates:change("menu")
end

function love.update(dt)
    gameStates:update(dt)
end

function love.draw()
    gameStates:draw()
end

function love.keypressed(key)
    gameStates:keypressed(key)
end

title: Entity Template
description: A reusable OOP entity base. Copy and rename for Player, Enemy, Bullet, etc.
code:
-- Player.lua  (rename as needed: Enemy, Bullet …)
-- Usage:
--   local Player = require("entities/Player")
--   local player = Player.new(100, 200)
--   -- in update: player:update(dt)
--   -- in draw:   player:draw()

local Player = {}
Player.__index = Player

function Player.new(x, y)
    local self = setmetatable({}, Player)
    self.x     = x or 0
    self.y     = y or 0
    self.speed = 140
    -- self.sprite = love.graphics.newImage("images/player.png")
    return self
end

function Player:update(dt)
    if love.keyboard.isDown("left",  "a") then self.x = self.x - self.speed * dt end
    if love.keyboard.isDown("right", "d") then self.x = self.x + self.speed * dt end
    if love.keyboard.isDown("up",    "w") then self.y = self.y - self.speed * dt end
    if love.keyboard.isDown("down",  "s") then self.y = self.y + self.speed * dt end
end

function Player:draw()
    -- love.graphics.draw(self.sprite, self.x, self.y)
    love.graphics.rectangle("line", self.x, self.y, 16, 16)
end

return Player

title: Camera
description: A simple 2D camera with smooth lerp follow. Save as entities/Camera.lua.
code:
-- Camera.lua
-- Usage:
--   local Camera = require("entities/Camera")
--   local cam = Camera.new()
--   -- in update: cam:follow(player, 0.1)
--   -- in draw:   cam:attach() ... draw world ... cam:detach()

local Camera = {}
Camera.__index = Camera

function Camera.new()
    return setmetatable({ x = 0, y = 0, zoom = 1 }, Camera)
end

-- Smoothly move camera toward target (any table with .x and .y).
-- lerp: 0 = never moves, 1 = instant snap (default 0.12)
function Camera:follow(target, lerp)
    lerp    = lerp or 0.12
    self.x  = self.x + (target.x - self.x) * lerp
    self.y  = self.y + (target.y - self.y) * lerp
end

-- Call before drawing world geometry.
function Camera:attach()
    local hw = love.graphics.getWidth()  / 2
    local hh = love.graphics.getHeight() / 2
    love.graphics.push()
    love.graphics.scale(self.zoom)
    love.graphics.translate(
        -math.floor(self.x - hw / self.zoom),
        -math.floor(self.y - hh / self.zoom)
    )
end

-- Call after drawing world geometry.
function Camera:detach()
    love.graphics.pop()
end

return Camera

title: Input Manager
description: Action-based input manager supporting both isDown() and wasPressed() (once per frame). Save as Input.lua.
code:
-- Input.lua
-- Usage:
--   local Input = require("Input")
--   -- forward love.keypressed to Input:
--   function love.keypressed(key) Input.keypressed(key) end
--   -- call at end of love.update:
--   Input.endFrame()
--
--   if Input.isDown("move_left")  then ... end  -- held
--   if Input.wasPressed("jump")   then ... end  -- single press

local Input = {
    bindings = {
        move_left  = { "a", "left" },
        move_right = { "d", "right" },
        move_up    = { "w", "up" },
        move_down  = { "s", "down" },
        jump       = { "space", "w" },
        shoot      = { "j", "lctrl" },
        pause      = { "escape", "p" },
    },
    _pressed = {},
}

function Input.keypressed(key)
    Input._pressed[key] = true
end

-- Call at the end of love.update(dt) to clear single-press state.
function Input.endFrame()
    Input._pressed = {}
end

-- True every frame while any bound key is held down.
function Input.isDown(action)
    for _, key in ipairs(Input.bindings[action] or {}) do
        if love.keyboard.isDown(key) then return true end
    end
    return false
end

-- True only on the first frame a bound key was pressed.
function Input.wasPressed(action)
    for _, key in ipairs(Input.bindings[action] or {}) do
        if Input._pressed[key] then return true end
    end
    return false
end

return Input

title: Audio Manager
description: Centralized audio manager for SFX and music with volume control and mute toggle. Save as AudioManager.lua.
code:
-- AudioManager.lua
-- Usage:
--   local Audio = require("AudioManager")
--   -- in love.load(): Audio.load()
--   Audio.play("jump")           -- play SFX
--   Audio.play("game_theme")     -- play/restart music
--   Audio.stop("game_theme")
--   Audio.setMusicVolume(0.5)
--   Audio.setSFXVolume(0.8)
--   Audio.toggleMute()

local AudioManager = {
    musicVolume = 1,
    sfxVolume   = 1,
    muted       = false,
    library = {
        -- Add your audio files here:
        -- name = { path = "audio/sound.wav", kind = "sfx",   loops = false, volume = 1.0 },
        -- name = { path = "audio/music.ogg", kind = "music", loops = true,  volume = 0.8 },
        jump       = { path = "audio/jump.wav",       kind = "sfx",   loops = false, volume = 1.0 },
        shoot      = { path = "audio/shoot.wav",      kind = "sfx",   loops = false, volume = 1.0 },
        menu_theme = { path = "audio/menu_theme.ogg", kind = "music", loops = true,  volume = 0.8 },
        game_theme = { path = "audio/game_theme.ogg", kind = "music", loops = true,  volume = 0.8 },
    },
    sources = {},
}

function AudioManager.load()
    for name, item in pairs(AudioManager.library) do
        local sourceType = item.kind == "music" and "stream" or "static"
        local src = love.audio.newSource(item.path, sourceType)
        src:setLooping(item.loops)
        AudioManager.sources[name] = src
    end
    AudioManager._refreshVolumes()
end

function AudioManager._refreshVolumes()
    for name, src in pairs(AudioManager.sources) do
        local item      = AudioManager.library[name]
        local baseVol   = item.kind == "music" and AudioManager.musicVolume or AudioManager.sfxVolume
        src:setVolume(AudioManager.muted and 0 or baseVol * item.volume)
    end
end

function AudioManager.play(name)
    local src = AudioManager.sources[name]
    if not src then return end
    if AudioManager.library[name].kind == "music" then
        src:stop()
        src:play()
    else
        src:clone():play()   -- allow overlapping SFX
    end
end

function AudioManager.stop(name)
    local src = AudioManager.sources[name]
    if src then src:stop() end
end

function AudioManager.setMusicVolume(v)
    AudioManager.musicVolume = v
    AudioManager._refreshVolumes()
end

function AudioManager.setSFXVolume(v)
    AudioManager.sfxVolume = v
    AudioManager._refreshVolumes()
end

function AudioManager.toggleMute()
    AudioManager.muted = not AudioManager.muted
    AudioManager._refreshVolumes()
end

return AudioManager

title: Basic Class
description: The simplest Lua class pattern using metatables. The foundation for everything else.
code:
-- Animal.lua
local Animal = {}
Animal.__index = Animal

-- Constructor
function Animal.new(name, sound)
    local self = setmetatable({}, Animal)
    self.name  = name
    self.sound = sound
    return self
end

-- Method
function Animal:speak()
    print(self.name .. " says " .. self.sound)
end

return Animal

-- Usage:
-- local Animal = require("Animal")
-- local dog = Animal.new("Rex", "Woof")
-- dog:speak()   --> Rex says Woof

title: Inheritance
description: Single inheritance in Lua - child class extends a parent class and can override methods.
code:
-- Base class
local Shape = {}
Shape.__index = Shape

function Shape.new(x, y)
    return setmetatable({ x = x, y = y }, Shape)
end

function Shape:move(dx, dy)
    self.x = self.x + dx
    self.y = self.y + dy
end

function Shape:draw()
    -- override in subclasses
end

-- Child class
local Circle = setmetatable({}, { __index = Shape })
Circle.__index = Circle

function Circle.new(x, y, radius)
    local self = Shape.new(x, y)     -- call parent constructor
    self.radius = radius
    return setmetatable(self, Circle)
end

function Circle:draw()               -- override parent method
    love.graphics.circle("fill", self.x, self.y, self.radius)
end

function Circle:area()
    return math.pi * self.radius ^ 2
end

return Circle

-- Usage:
-- local Circle = require("Circle")
-- local c = Circle.new(100, 100, 30)
-- c:move(10, 0)    -- inherited from Shape
-- c:draw()         -- Circle's own draw

title: Mixin
description: Copy methods from multiple tables into a class - Lua's answer to multiple inheritance.
code:
-- Mixins are plain tables of functions
local Drawable = {}
function Drawable:draw()
    love.graphics.rectangle("fill", self.x, self.y, self.w, self.h)
end

local Collidable = {}
function Collidable:collidesWith(other)
    return self.x < other.x + other.w
       and self.x + self.w > other.x
       and self.y < other.y + other.h
       and self.y + self.h > other.y
end

-- Helper: copy mixin methods into a class
local function mixin(class, ...)
    for _, m in ipairs({...}) do
        for k, v in pairs(m) do
            class[k] = v
        end
    end
end

-- Class that uses both mixins
local Box = {}
Box.__index = Box
mixin(Box, Drawable, Collidable)

function Box.new(x, y, w, h)
    return setmetatable({ x=x, y=y, w=w, h=h }, Box)
end

return Box

-- Usage:
-- local Box = require("Box")
-- local a = Box.new(0,  0, 32, 32)
-- local b = Box.new(20, 0, 32, 32)
-- a:draw()
-- print(a:collidesWith(b))  --> true

title: Class() Helper Function
description: A tiny reusable Class() helper that handles metatable boilerplate so you don't repeat it every time.
code:
-- Class.lua  - save once, use everywhere
local function Class(Base)
    local cls = {}
    cls.__index = cls
    if Base then
        setmetatable(cls, { __index = Base })
    end
    function cls:new(...)      -- calls cls:init(...) if it exists
        local obj = setmetatable({}, cls)
        if obj.init then obj:init(...) end
        return obj
    end
    return cls
end

return Class

-- Usage:
-- local Class = require("Class")
--
-- local Animal = Class()
-- function Animal:init(name)
--     self.name = name
-- end
-- function Animal:speak() print(self.name) end
--
-- local Dog = Class(Animal)   -- inherits Animal
-- function Dog:init(name)
--     Animal.init(self, name) -- call super
--     self.tricks = {}
-- end
-- function Dog:learnTrick(t) table.insert(self.tricks, t) end
--
-- local d = Dog:new("Rex")
-- d:speak()              --> Rex
-- d:learnTrick("sit")

title: Singleton Module
description: A module that has exactly one instance - useful for global managers (GameManager, EventBus, etc.).
code:
-- GameManager.lua
local GameManager = {}
GameManager.__index = GameManager

-- Private single instance
local _instance = nil

function GameManager.getInstance()
    if not _instance then
        _instance = setmetatable({
            score  = 0,
            lives  = 3,
            paused = false,
        }, GameManager)
    end
    return _instance
end

function GameManager:addScore(points)
    self.score = self.score + points
end

function GameManager:loseLife()
    self.lives = self.lives - 1
    return self.lives <= 0   -- returns true = game over
end

function GameManager:reset()
    self.score  = 0
    self.lives  = 3
    self.paused = false
end

return GameManager

-- Usage (any file):
-- local GM = require("GameManager").getInstance()
-- GM:addScore(100)
-- if GM:loseLife() then ... game over ... end

title: Event Emitter
description: Observer pattern - objects subscribe to named events and get notified when they fire.
code:
-- EventEmitter.lua
local EventEmitter = {}
EventEmitter.__index = EventEmitter

function EventEmitter.new()
    return setmetatable({ _listeners = {} }, EventEmitter)
end

function EventEmitter:on(event, fn)
    if not self._listeners[event] then
        self._listeners[event] = {}
    end
    table.insert(self._listeners[event], fn)
end

function EventEmitter:off(event, fn)
    local list = self._listeners[event]
    if not list then return end
    for i = #list, 1, -1 do
        if list[i] == fn then table.remove(list, i) end
    end
end

function EventEmitter:emit(event, ...)
    for _, fn in ipairs(self._listeners[event] or {}) do
        fn(...)
    end
end

return EventEmitter

-- Usage:
-- local EventEmitter = require("EventEmitter")
-- local events = EventEmitter.new()
--
-- events:on("playerDied", function(player)
--     print(player.name .. " died!")
-- end)
--
-- events:emit("playerDied", player)   -- fires all listeners

title: Component / Entity System
description: Simple composition-over-inheritance entity-component system. Attach components to entities instead of deep class trees.
code:
-- Entity.lua
local Entity = {}
Entity.__index = Entity

function Entity.new()
    return setmetatable({ _components = {} }, Entity)
end

function Entity:add(name, component)
    self._components[name] = component
    component.entity = self   -- back-reference
    if component.init then component:init() end
    return self               -- chain: e:add("hp", HP.new()):add("sprite", Sprite.new())
end

function Entity:get(name)
    return self._components[name]
end

function Entity:update(dt)
    for _, c in pairs(self._components) do
        if c.update then c:update(dt) end
    end
end

function Entity:draw()
    for _, c in pairs(self._components) do
        if c.draw then c:draw() end
    end
end

return Entity

-- Example component:
-- local Health = {}
-- Health.__index = Health
-- function Health.new(max) return setmetatable({ hp = max, max = max }, Health) end
-- function Health:takeDamage(n) self.hp = math.max(0, self.hp - n) end

title: Object Pool
description: Reuse objects instead of creating/GC-ing them every frame - essential for bullets, particles, and enemies.
code:
-- Pool.lua
local Pool = {}
Pool.__index = Pool

function Pool.new(Class)
    return setmetatable({ Class = Class, free = {} }, Pool)
end

function Pool:get(...)
    local obj
    if #self.free > 0 then
        obj = table.remove(self.free)
        if obj.reset then obj:reset(...) end
    else
        obj = self.Class.new(...)
    end
    obj._pool = self
    return obj
end

function Pool:release(obj)
    obj._pool = nil
    table.insert(self.free, obj)
end

return Pool

-- Usage:
-- local Pool   = require("Pool")
-- local Bullet = require("Bullet")   -- Bullet.new() / bullet:reset(x,y)
-- local bullets = Pool.new(Bullet)
--
-- local b = bullets:get(player.x, player.y)  -- borrow
-- -- when bullet hits something:
-- bullets:release(b)                          -- return to pool

title: Vector2
description: 2D vector class with common operations: add, sub, scale, length, normalize, dot, distance.
code:
-- Vec2.lua
local Vec2 = {}
Vec2.__index = Vec2

function Vec2.new(x, y)
    return setmetatable({ x = x or 0, y = y or 0 }, Vec2)
end

function Vec2.__add(a, b)  return Vec2.new(a.x + b.x, a.y + b.y) end
function Vec2.__sub(a, b)  return Vec2.new(a.x - b.x, a.y - b.y) end
function Vec2.__mul(a, s)  return Vec2.new(a.x * s,   a.y * s)   end
function Vec2.__unm(a)     return Vec2.new(-a.x, -a.y)            end
function Vec2.__tostring(v) return "(" .. v.x .. ", " .. v.y .. ")" end

function Vec2:length()     return math.sqrt(self.x^2 + self.y^2) end
function Vec2:lengthSq()   return self.x^2 + self.y^2            end

function Vec2:normalize()
    local len = self:length()
    if len == 0 then return Vec2.new(0, 0) end
    return Vec2.new(self.x / len, self.y / len)
end

function Vec2:dot(other)   return self.x * other.x + self.y * other.y end
function Vec2:distance(other) return (self - other):length() end
function Vec2:angle()      return math.atan2(self.y, self.x) end
function Vec2:clone()      return Vec2.new(self.x, self.y)   end

return Vec2

-- Usage:
-- local v1 = Vec2.new(3, 4)
-- local v2 = Vec2.new(1, 0)
-- print(v1:length())          --> 5
-- print(v1:normalize())       --> (0.6, 0.8)
-- print(v1:dot(v2))           --> 3
-- local v3 = v1 + v2          --> (4, 4)

title: Math Utilities
description: Commonly needed math helpers: lerp, clamp, map, sign, round, and random in range.

title: Easing Functions
description: Common easing curves for smooth animations and tweens. t is always 0..1.
code:
-- Easing.lua  (t = 0..1)
local Easing = {}

function Easing.linear(t)     return t end

function Easing.inQuad(t)     return t * t end
function Easing.outQuad(t)    return t * (2 - t) end
function Easing.inOutQuad(t)
    if t < 0.5 then return 2*t*t else return -1 + (4-2*t)*t end
end

function Easing.inCubic(t)    return t * t * t end
function Easing.outCubic(t)   local s = t-1; return s*s*s + 1 end
function Easing.inOutCubic(t)
    if t < 0.5 then return 4*t*t*t else local s=2*t-2; return 1 + s*s*s/2 end
end

function Easing.outBounce(t)
    if t < 1/2.75 then
        return 7.5625 * t * t
    elseif t < 2/2.75 then
        t = t - 1.5/2.75; return 7.5625*t*t + 0.75
    elseif t < 2.5/2.75 then
        t = t - 2.25/2.75; return 7.5625*t*t + 0.9375
    else
        t = t - 2.625/2.75; return 7.5625*t*t + 0.984375
    end
end

function Easing.outElastic(t)
    if t == 0 or t == 1 then return t end
    return 2^(-10*t) * math.sin((t*10 - 0.75) * (2*math.pi/3)) + 1
end

function Easing.outBack(t)
    local c = 1.70158 + 1
    return 1 + c * (t-1)^3 + 1.70158 * (t-1)^2
end

return Easing

-- Usage:
-- x = startX + (endX - startX) * Easing.outBounce(timer / duration)

title: AABB Collision
description: Axis-Aligned Bounding Box overlap test - no physics engine needed.
code:
-- Returns true if two axis-aligned rectangles overlap
local function aabb(ax, ay, aw, ah, bx, by, bw, bh)
    return ax < bx + bw
       and ax + aw > bx
       and ay < by + bh
       and ay + ah > by
end

-- Returns overlap depth on each axis (for resolution)
local function aabbOverlap(ax, ay, aw, ah, bx, by, bw, bh)
    local ox = math.min(ax+aw, bx+bw) - math.max(ax, bx)
    local oy = math.min(ay+ah, by+bh) - math.max(ay, by)
    if ox <= 0 or oy <= 0 then return false, 0, 0 end
    return true, ox, oy
end

-- Simple push-out resolution
local function resolveAabb(ax, ay, aw, ah, bx, by, bw, bh)
    local hit, ox, oy = aabbOverlap(ax, ay, aw, ah, bx, by, bw, bh)
    if not hit then return ax, ay end
    if ox < oy then
        ax = ax + (ax < bx and -ox or ox)
    else
        ay = ay + (ay < by and -oy or oy)
    end
    return ax, ay
end

title: Circle Collision
description: Circle-circle and circle-rectangle overlap tests.
code:
-- Circle vs Circle
local function circleCircle(ax, ay, ar, bx, by, br)
    local dx = ax - bx
    local dy = ay - by
    return dx*dx + dy*dy < (ar + br)^2
end

-- Circle vs AABB  (returns true + nearest point on rect)
local function circleRect(cx, cy, cr, rx, ry, rw, rh)
    local nearX = math.max(rx, math.min(cx, rx + rw))
    local nearY = math.max(ry, math.min(cy, ry + rh))
    local dx = cx - nearX
    local dy = cy - nearY
    return dx*dx + dy*dy < cr*cr, nearX, nearY
end

-- Point inside circle
local function pointInCircle(px, py, cx, cy, cr)
    return (px-cx)^2 + (py-cy)^2 < cr*cr
end

title: Ray vs AABB
description: Cast a ray (origin + direction) against a rectangle. Returns hit distance t.
code:
-- Ray vs AABB (slab method)
-- ox,oy  = ray origin
-- dx,dy  = ray direction (does NOT need to be normalized)
-- rx,ry,rw,rh = rectangle
-- Returns: hit (bool), t (distance along ray to entry point)
local function rayVsAabb(ox, oy, dx, dy, rx, ry, rw, rh)
    local tminX = (rx      - ox) / dx
    local tmaxX = (rx + rw - ox) / dx
    local tminY = (ry      - oy) / dy
    local tmaxY = (ry + rh - oy) / dy

    if dx == 0 then tminX = -math.huge; tmaxX = math.huge end
    if dy == 0 then tminY = -math.huge; tmaxY = math.huge end

    local tmin = math.max(math.min(tminX, tmaxX), math.min(tminY, tmaxY))
    local tmax = math.min(math.max(tminX, tmaxX), math.max(tminY, tmaxY))

    if tmax < 0 or tmin > tmax then return false, math.huge end
    return true, math.max(tmin, 0)
end

title: Tween
description: Lightweight single-value tween with an optional easing function and on-complete callback.
code:
-- Tween.lua
local Tween = {}
Tween.__index = Tween

function Tween.new(from, to, duration, easing, onComplete)
    return setmetatable({
        value      = from,
        from       = from,
        to         = to,
        duration   = duration,
        elapsed    = 0,
        easing     = easing or function(t) return t end,
        onComplete = onComplete,
        done       = false,
    }, Tween)
end

function Tween:update(dt)
    if self.done then return end
    self.elapsed = math.min(self.elapsed + dt, self.duration)
    local t = self.elapsed / self.duration
    self.value = self.from + (self.to - self.from) * self.easing(t)
    if self.elapsed >= self.duration then
        self.done = true
        if self.onComplete then self.onComplete() end
    end
end

return Tween

title: Tween Table Properties
description: Tween multiple numeric fields on a table at once - animate position, color, scale, etc.
code:
-- TweenTable.lua
local TweenTable = {}
TweenTable.__index = TweenTable

function TweenTable.new(target, goals, duration, easing, onComplete)
    local starts = {}
    for k, v in pairs(goals) do starts[k] = target[k] end
    return setmetatable({
        target     = target,
        goals      = goals,
        starts     = starts,
        duration   = duration,
        elapsed    = 0,
        easing     = easing or function(t) return t end,
        onComplete = onComplete,
        done       = false,
    }, TweenTable)
end

function TweenTable:update(dt)
    if self.done then return end
    self.elapsed = math.min(self.elapsed + dt, self.duration)
    local t = self.easing(self.elapsed / self.duration)
    for k, goal in pairs(self.goals) do
        self.target[k] = self.starts[k] + (goal - self.starts[k]) * t
    end
    if self.elapsed >= self.duration then
        self.done = true
        if self.onComplete then self.onComplete() end
    end
end

return TweenTable

-- Button.lua
local Button = {}
Button.__index = Button

function Button.new(x, y, w, h, label, onClick)
    return setmetatable({
        x = x, y = y, w = w, h = h,
        label   = label,
        onClick = onClick,
        hovered = false,
        pressed = false,
    }, Button)
end

function Button:containsPoint(px, py)
    return px >= self.x and px <= self.x + self.w
       and py >= self.y and py <= self.y + self.h
end

function Button:update()
    local mx, my = love.mouse.getPosition()
    self.hovered = self:containsPoint(mx, my)
end

function Button:mousepressed(x, y, btn)
    if btn == 1 and self:containsPoint(x, y) then
        self.pressed = true
    end
end

function Button:mousereleased(x, y, btn)
    if btn == 1 and self.pressed and self:containsPoint(x, y) then
        if self.onClick then self.onClick() end
    end
    self.pressed = false
end

function Button:draw()
    if self.pressed then
        love.graphics.setColor(0.3, 0.5, 0.9)
    elseif self.hovered then
        love.graphics.setColor(0.4, 0.6, 1.0)
    else
        love.graphics.setColor(0.25, 0.35, 0.7)
    end
    love.graphics.rectangle("fill", self.x, self.y, self.w, self.h, 6, 6)
    love.graphics.setColor(1, 1, 1)
    love.graphics.printf(self.label, self.x, self.y + self.h/2 - 7, self.w, "center")
end

return Button

description: Animated progress bar for health, mana, XP, loading - any normalized value 0..1.
code:
-- ProgressBar.lua
local ProgressBar = {}
ProgressBar.__index = ProgressBar

function ProgressBar.new(x, y, w, h, opts)
    opts = opts or {}
    return setmetatable({
        x = x, y = y, w = w, h = h,
        value        = opts.value        or 1,    -- current 0..1
        displayValue = opts.value        or 1,    -- animated display
        bgColor      = opts.bgColor      or {0.15, 0.15, 0.15},
        fillColor    = opts.fillColor    or {0.2, 0.8, 0.3},
        borderColor  = opts.borderColor  or {0.5, 0.5, 0.5},
        cornerRadius = opts.cornerRadius or 4,
        lerpSpeed    = opts.lerpSpeed    or 4,    -- 0 = instant
    }, ProgressBar)
end

function ProgressBar:setValue(v)
    self.value = math.max(0, math.min(1, v))
end

function ProgressBar:update(dt)
    if self.lerpSpeed > 0 then
        self.displayValue = self.displayValue + (self.value - self.displayValue) * self.lerpSpeed * dt
    else
        self.displayValue = self.value
    end
end

function ProgressBar:draw()
    local r = self.cornerRadius
    love.graphics.setColor(self.bgColor)
    love.graphics.rectangle("fill", self.x, self.y, self.w, self.h, r, r)
    if self.displayValue > 0 then
        love.graphics.setColor(self.fillColor)
        love.graphics.rectangle("fill", self.x, self.y, self.w * self.displayValue, self.h, r, r)
    end
    love.graphics.setColor(self.borderColor)
    love.graphics.rectangle("line", self.x, self.y, self.w, self.h, r, r)
    love.graphics.setColor(1,1,1)
end

return ProgressBar

title: Debug Grid
description: Draw a world-space grid - useful for tile alignment and camera debugging.

local function drawGrid(cellSize, camX, camY, color)
    cellSize = cellSize or 32
    camX     = camX     or 0
    camY     = camY     or 0
    color    = color    or {1, 1, 1, 0.08}

    local W = love.graphics.getWidth()
    local H = love.graphics.getHeight()

    local startX = math.floor(camX / cellSize) * cellSize - camX
    local startY = math.floor(camY / cellSize) * cellSize - camY

    love.graphics.setColor(color)
    local x = startX
    while x <= W do
        love.graphics.line(x, 0, x, H)
        x = x + cellSize
    end
    local y = startY
    while y <= H do
        love.graphics.line(0, y, W, y)
        y = y + cellSize
    end
    love.graphics.setColor(1,1,1)
end

title: Stencil Mask
description: Use a stencil to mask drawing to a specific shape - good for portals, flashlights, UI cutouts.
code:
function love.draw()
    -- 1. Write the mask shape into the stencil buffer
    love.graphics.stencil(function()
        love.graphics.circle("fill", 400, 300, 120)  -- circular viewport
    end, "replace", 1)

    -- 2. Only draw where stencil == 1
    love.graphics.setStencilTest("equal", 1)
        love.graphics.setColor(0.2, 0.6, 1)
        love.graphics.rectangle("fill", 0, 0, 800, 600)  -- fills only inside circle
        love.graphics.setColor(1, 1, 1)
        love.graphics.print("Inside the mask", 330, 290)
    love.graphics.setStencilTest()  -- disable stencil test

    -- 3. Draw outside the mask (invert test)
    love.graphics.stencil(function()
        love.graphics.circle("fill", 400, 300, 120)
    end, "replace", 1)
    love.graphics.setStencilTest("notequal", 1)
        love.graphics.setColor(0.08, 0.08, 0.08, 0.85)
        love.graphics.rectangle("fill", 0, 0, 800, 600)  -- dim outside
    love.graphics.setStencilTest()
end

title: Spritesheet Animation
description: Frame-by-frame animation from a spritesheet using Quads. Supports multiple named animations.
code:
-- Animator.lua
local Animator = {}
Animator.__index = Animator

function Animator.new(image, frameW, frameH)
    local iw, ih = image:getDimensions()
    local self = setmetatable({
        image   = image,
        frameW  = frameW,
        frameH  = frameH,
        anims   = {},
        current = nil,
        frame   = 1,
        timer   = 0,
    }, Animator)
    -- Pre-build all quads
    self.quads = {}
    local idx = 1
    for row = 0, ih / frameH - 1 do
        for col = 0, iw / frameW - 1 do
            self.quads[idx] = love.graphics.newQuad(
                col*frameW, row*frameH, frameW, frameH, iw, ih)
            idx = idx + 1
        end
    end
    return self
end

-- frames: list of quad indices, fps: playback speed
function Animator:addAnim(name, frames, fps, loop)
    self.anims[name] = { frames=frames, fps=fps or 8, loop=loop~=false }
end

function Animator:play(name)
    if self.current == name then return end
    self.current = name
    self.frame   = 1
    self.timer   = 0
end

function Animator:update(dt)
    local anim = self.anims[self.current]
    if not anim then return end
    self.timer = self.timer + dt
    if self.timer >= 1 / anim.fps then
        self.timer = 0
        self.frame = self.frame + 1
        if self.frame > #anim.frames then
            self.frame = anim.loop and 1 or #anim.frames
        end
    end
end

function Animator:draw(x, y, r, sx, sy)
    local anim = self.anims[self.current]
    if not anim then return end
    local quad = self.quads[anim.frames[self.frame]]
    love.graphics.draw(self.image, quad, x, y, r or 0, sx or 1, sy or 1,
        self.frameW/2, self.frameH/2)
end

return Animator

local gamepad = nil

function love.load()
    -- Use first connected joystick if available
    local joysticks = love.joystick.getJoysticks()
    if joysticks[1] and joysticks[1]:isGamepad() then
        gamepad = joysticks[1]
    end
end

function love.joystickadded(joystick)
    if joystick:isGamepad() and not gamepad then
        gamepad = joystick
        print("Gamepad connected:", joystick:getName())
    end
end

function love.joystickremoved(joystick)
    if joystick == gamepad then
        gamepad = nil
        print("Gamepad disconnected")
    end
end

function love.update(dt)
    if not gamepad then return end

    -- Buttons (A/B/X/Y, dpads, shoulders)
    if gamepad:isGamepadDown("a") then print("A pressed") end
    if gamepad:isGamepadDown("dpup") then print("D-pad up") end

    -- Axes: leftx, lefty, rightx, righty, triggerleft, triggerright (-1..1)
    local lx = gamepad:getGamepadAxis("leftx")
    local ly = gamepad:getGamepadAxis("lefty")
    local deadzone = 0.15
    if math.abs(lx) > deadzone then
        player.x = player.x + lx * player.speed * dt
    end
    if math.abs(ly) > deadzone then
        player.y = player.y + ly * player.speed * dt
    end

    -- Triggers (0..1)
    local rt = gamepad:getGamepadAxis("triggerright")
    if rt > 0.5 then print("Shooting!") end
end

end

local sfx, music

function love.load()
    -- Reverb effect (simulates room acoustics)
    love.audio.setEffect("reverb", {
        type    = "reverb",
        volume  = 1.0,
        decaytime = 1.5,    -- seconds
        density = 0.9,
        diffusion = 0.8,
    })

    -- Low-pass filter (muffled sound, e.g. through a wall)
    love.audio.setEffect("muffled", {
        type    = "lowpass",
        volume  = 1.0,
        highgain = 0.1,    -- 0 = fully muffled, 1 = no filter
    })

    sfx   = love.audio.newSource("sounds/jump.wav", "static")
    music = love.audio.newSource("music/theme.ogg", "stream")
    music:setLooping(true)
    music:play()
end

function love.keypressed(key)
    if key == "1" then
        sfx:setEffect("reverb")
        sfx:stop(); sfx:play()
    elseif key == "2" then
        sfx:setEffect("muffled")
        sfx:stop(); sfx:play()
    elseif key == "3" then
        sfx:setEffect(nil)   -- remove effects
        sfx:stop(); sfx:play()
    end
    -- Toggle muffled music (e.g. entering a building)
    if key == "m" then
        music:setEffect(music:getEffect() and nil or "muffled")
    end
end

local explosion

function love.load()
    explosion = love.audio.newSource("sounds/explosion.wav", "static")

    -- Attenuation model: sound fades beyond referenceDistance
    explosion:setAttenuationDistances(80, 500)  -- ref, max
    explosion:setRolloff(1.0)   -- 1 = linear falloff
end

function love.update(dt)
    -- Move listener with arrow keys
    if love.keyboard.isDown("left")  then listenerX = listenerX - 200*dt end
    if love.keyboard.isDown("right") then listenerX = listenerX + 200*dt end
    if love.keyboard.isDown("up")    then listenerY = listenerY - 200*dt end
    if love.keyboard.isDown("down")  then listenerY = listenerY + 200*dt end

    -- LÖVE uses 3D internally; map 2D coords to x,z plane
    love.audio.setPosition(listenerX / 100, 0, listenerY / 100)
end

function love.mousepressed(x, y, btn)
    if btn == 1 then
        -- Play explosion at clicked position
        local src = explosion:clone()
        src:setPosition(x / 100, 0, y / 100)
        src:setRelative(false)
        src:play()
    end
end

title: Physics Collision Callbacks
description: Detect when Box2D fixtures begin and end contact - react to collisions in game logic.
code:
local world, playerBody, groundBody
local isGrounded = false

function love.load()
    world = love.physics.newWorld(0, 9.81 * 64, true)

    -- Ground
    groundBody    = love.physics.newBody(world, 400, 480, "static")
    local gShape  = love.physics.newRectangleShape(800, 40)
    local gFix    = love.physics.newFixture(groundBody, gShape)
    gFix:setUserData("ground")

    -- Player
    playerBody    = love.physics.newBody(world, 200, 100, "dynamic")
    local pShape  = love.physics.newRectangleShape(30, 50)
    local pFix    = love.physics.newFixture(playerBody, pShape, 1)
    pFix:setUserData("player")
    playerBody:setFixedRotation(true)

    -- Collision callbacks
    world:setCallbacks(
        function(a, b, coll)  -- beginContact
            local da, db = a:getUserData(), b:getUserData()
            if (da=="player" and db=="ground") or
               (da=="ground" and db=="player") then
                isGrounded = true
            end
        end,
        function(a, b, coll)  -- endContact
            local da, db = a:getUserData(), b:getUserData()
            if (da=="player" and db=="ground") or
               (da=="ground" and db=="player") then
                isGrounded = false
            end
        end,
        nil, nil              -- preSolve, postSolve
    )
end

function love.update(dt)
    world:update(dt)
end

function love.keypressed(key)
    if key == "space" and isGrounded then
        playerBody:applyLinearImpulse(0, -400)
    end
end

title: Physics Joints

local world, bodyA, bodyB
local distJoint, revJoint, mouseJoint
local anchor

function love.load()
    world = love.physics.newWorld(0, 400, true)

    local shapeA = love.physics.newRectangleShape(40, 40)
    local shapeB = love.physics.newRectangleShape(40, 40)

    bodyA = love.physics.newBody(world, 300, 200, "dynamic")
    love.physics.newFixture(bodyA, shapeA, 1)

    bodyB = love.physics.newBody(world, 450, 200, "dynamic")
    love.physics.newFixture(bodyB, shapeB, 1)

    -- Distance joint: keeps two bodies a fixed distance apart
    distJoint = love.physics.newDistanceJoint(bodyA, bodyB,
        bodyA:getX(), bodyA:getY(),
        bodyB:getX(), bodyB:getY(), true)
    distJoint:setFrequency(2)     -- springiness
    distJoint:setDampingRatio(0.5)

    -- Revolute joint: pin bodyB to a static anchor (like a pendulum)
    anchor = love.physics.newBody(world, 400, 100, "static")
    local anchorShape = love.physics.newCircleShape(5)
    love.physics.newFixture(anchor, anchorShape)
    revJoint = love.physics.newRevoluteJoint(anchor, bodyB,
        anchor:getX(), anchor:getY(), true)
    -- revJoint:setMotorEnabled(true)
    -- revJoint:setMotorSpeed(2)
    -- revJoint:setMaxMotorTorque(1000)
end

title: Lua Table Serializer
description: Serialize and deserialize a Lua table to/from a string - a minimal save system without external libraries.
code:
-- Serialize.lua
local Serialize = {}

function Serialize.encode(val, indent)
    local t = type(val)
    if t == "number"  then return tostring(val)
    elseif t == "boolean" then return tostring(val)
    elseif t == "string"  then return string.format("%q", val)
    elseif t == "table" then
        local items = {}
        for k, v in pairs(val) do
            local key = type(k) == "string"
                and string.format("[%q]=", k)
                or  "["..tostring(k).."]="
            table.insert(items, key .. Serialize.encode(v))
        end
        return "{" .. table.concat(items, ",") .. "}"
    end
    return "nil"
end

function Serialize.decode(str)
    local fn, err = load("return " .. str)
    if not fn then error("Deserialize error: " .. err) end
    return fn()
end

function Serialize.save(filename, data)
    local str = Serialize.encode(data)
    love.filesystem.write(filename, str)
end

function Serialize.load(filename)
    if not love.filesystem.getInfo(filename) then return nil end
    local str = love.filesystem.read(filename)
    return Serialize.decode(str)
end

return Serialize

-- List files in a directory
local function listFiles(dir, ext)
    local results = {}
    local items = love.filesystem.getDirectoryItems(dir)
    for _, name in ipairs(items) do
        local path = dir .. "/" .. name
        local info = love.filesystem.getInfo(path)
        if info then
            if info.type == "file" then
                if not ext or name:match("%" .. ext .. "$") then
                    table.insert(results, { path=path, name=name, size=info.size })
                end
            end
        end
    end
    return results
end

-- Recursive file walker
local function walkDir(dir, callback)
    for _, name in ipairs(love.filesystem.getDirectoryItems(dir)) do
        local path = dir .. "/" .. name
        local info = love.filesystem.getInfo(path)
        if info then
            if info.type == "directory" then
                walkDir(path, callback)
            elseif info.type == "file" then
                callback(path, name, info)
            end
        end
    end
end

-- Vertical gradient (top color -> bottom color)
local function gradientRect(x, y, w, h, colorTop, colorBot)
    local verts = {
        { x,   y,   0, 0, colorTop[1], colorTop[2], colorTop[3], colorTop[4] or 1 },
        { x+w, y,   1, 0, colorTop[1], colorTop[2], colorTop[3], colorTop[4] or 1 },
        { x+w, y+h, 1, 1, colorBot[1], colorBot[2], colorBot[3], colorBot[4] or 1 },
        { x,   y+h, 0, 1, colorBot[1], colorBot[2], colorBot[3], colorBot[4] or 1 },
    }
    local mesh = love.graphics.newMesh(verts, "fan")
    love.graphics.draw(mesh)
end

-- Radial gradient from center
local function radialGradient(cx, cy, radius, colorIn, colorOut, steps)
    steps = steps or 32
    local verts = {}
    -- Center vertex
    table.insert(verts, { cx, cy, 0.5, 0.5,
        colorIn[1], colorIn[2], colorIn[3], colorIn[4] or 1 })
    for i = 0, steps do
        local a = (i / steps) * 2 * math.pi
        table.insert(verts, {
            cx + math.cos(a) * radius,
            cy + math.sin(a) * radius,
            0.5 + 0.5*math.cos(a),
            0.5 + 0.5*math.sin(a),
            colorOut[1], colorOut[2], colorOut[3], colorOut[4] or 1
        })
    end
    love.graphics.draw(love.graphics.newMesh(verts, "fan"))
end

title: Timer Manager
description: Schedule one-shot and repeating callbacks by name - no manual timer tracking.
code:
-- Timer.lua
local Timer = {}
Timer.__index = Timer

function Timer.new()
    return setmetatable({ _timers = {} }, Timer)
end

-- Call fn after `delay` seconds (once)
function Timer:after(delay, fn, tag)
    table.insert(self._timers, { t=delay, fn=fn, tag=tag, repeat_=false })
end

-- Call fn every `interval` seconds
function Timer:every(interval, fn, tag)
    table.insert(self._timers, { t=interval, interval=interval, fn=fn, tag=tag, repeat_=true })
end

-- Cancel all timers with a given tag
function Timer:cancel(tag)
    for i = #self._timers, 1, -1 do
        if self._timers[i].tag == tag then
            table.remove(self._timers, i)
        end
    end
end

function Timer:update(dt)
    for i = #self._timers, 1, -1 do
        local tmr = self._timers[i]
        tmr.t = tmr.t - dt
        if tmr.t <= 0 then
            tmr.fn()
            if tmr.repeat_ then
                tmr.t = tmr.interval
            else
                table.remove(self._timers, i)
            end
        end
    end
end

return Timer

-- timers:every(0.5, function() shootBullet() end, "shoot")

title: Screen Shake
description: Trauma-based camera shake. Add trauma on impact; shake intensity falls off as trauma squared.
code:
-- ScreenShake.lua
local ScreenShake = {
    trauma    = 0,
    maxOffset = 18,
    maxAngle  = 0.06,  -- radians
    decay     = 1.8,
    offsetX   = 0,
    offsetY   = 0,
    angle     = 0,
}

function ScreenShake.add(amount)
    ScreenShake.trauma = math.min(ScreenShake.trauma + amount, 1)
end

function ScreenShake.update(dt)
    ScreenShake.trauma = math.max(ScreenShake.trauma - ScreenShake.decay * dt, 0)
    local s = ScreenShake.trauma ^ 2
    local r = love.math.random
    ScreenShake.offsetX = ScreenShake.maxOffset * s * (r()*2 - 1)
    ScreenShake.offsetY = ScreenShake.maxOffset * s * (r()*2 - 1)
    ScreenShake.angle   = ScreenShake.maxAngle  * s * (r()*2 - 1)
end

function ScreenShake.attach()
    local hw = love.graphics.getWidth()  / 2
    local hh = love.graphics.getHeight() / 2
    love.graphics.push()
    love.graphics.translate(hw + ScreenShake.offsetX, hh + ScreenShake.offsetY)
    love.graphics.rotate(ScreenShake.angle)
    love.graphics.translate(-hw, -hh)
end

function ScreenShake.detach()
    love.graphics.pop()
end

return ScreenShake

-- Usage:
-- ScreenShake.add(0.6)  -- on explosion, bullet hit, etc.
-- -- in update: ScreenShake.update(dt)
-- -- in draw:
--   ScreenShake.attach()
--     -- draw world
--   ScreenShake.detach()
--     -- draw HUD (unaffected)

title: Grid / Tilemap Data Structure
description: 2D grid with get/set, world-to-cell conversion, and neighbor queries.
code:
-- Grid.lua
local Grid = {}
Grid.__index = Grid

function Grid.new(cols, rows, tileSize, default)
    local self = setmetatable({
        cols     = cols,
        rows     = rows,
        tileSize = tileSize or 32,
        _data    = {},
    }, Grid)
    for y = 1, rows do
        self._data[y] = {}
        for x = 1, cols do
            self._data[y][x] = default or 0
        end
    end
    return self
end

function Grid:inBounds(x, y)
    return x >= 1 and x <= self.cols and y >= 1 and y <= self.rows
end

function Grid:get(x, y)        if self:inBounds(x,y) then return self._data[y][x] end end
function Grid:set(x, y, val)   if self:inBounds(x,y) then self._data[y][x] = val  end end

-- Convert world pixels -> cell coords
function Grid:worldToCell(wx, wy)
    return math.floor(wx / self.tileSize) + 1,
           math.floor(wy / self.tileSize) + 1
end

-- Convert cell -> world pixel (top-left corner)
function Grid:cellToWorld(cx, cy)
    return (cx-1) * self.tileSize, (cy-1) * self.tileSize
end

-- 4-directional neighbors
function Grid:neighbors4(x, y)
    local n = {}
    for _, d in ipairs({{0,-1},{1,0},{0,1},{-1,0}}) do
        local nx, ny = x+d[1], y+d[2]
        if self:inBounds(nx, ny) then
            table.insert(n, { x=nx, y=ny, val=self:get(nx,ny) })
        end
    end
    return n
end

function Grid:forEach(fn)
    for y = 1, self.rows do
        for x = 1, self.cols do
            fn(x, y, self._data[y][x])
        end
    end
end

return Grid

title: State Sync + Interpolation
description: Server broadcasts world state at a fixed tick rate; clients buffer and interpolate between snapshots for smooth rendering.
code:
-- CLIENT SIDE
-- Snapshot buffer - store last two received states
local snapshots = {}   -- { time, entities: {id -> {x,y}} }
local INTERP_DELAY = 0.1  -- render 100ms behind server

local function receiveSnapshot(data)
    -- data is a table decoded from network message
    table.insert(snapshots, { time = data.serverTime, entities = data.entities })
    -- Keep only last 3 snapshots
    if #snapshots > 3 then table.remove(snapshots, 1) end
end

local function getInterpolated(renderTime)
    -- Find two snapshots to interpolate between
    local prev, next
    for i = #snapshots, 2, -1 do
        if snapshots[i-1].time <= renderTime and snapshots[i].time >= renderTime then
            prev = snapshots[i-1]
            next = snapshots[i]
            break
        end
    end
    if not prev or not next then
        return snapshots[#snapshots] and snapshots[#snapshots].entities or {}
    end
    local t = (renderTime - prev.time) / (next.time - prev.time)
    t = math.max(0, math.min(1, t))
    local result = {}
    for id, e in pairs(next.entities) do
        local p = prev.entities[id]
        if p then
            result[id] = {
                x = p.x + (e.x - p.x) * t,
                y = p.y + (e.y - p.y) * t,
            }
        else
            result[id] = e
        end
    end
    return result
end

local serverTimeOffset = 0   -- calibrate with server clock sync
local clientTime = 0

function love.update(dt)
    clientTime = clientTime + dt
    -- receive and buffer incoming snapshots here...
end

function love.draw()
    local renderTime = clientTime - INTERP_DELAY + serverTimeOffset
    local state = getInterpolated(renderTime)
    for id, e in pairs(state) do
        love.graphics.rectangle("fill", e.x, e.y, 24, 24)
    end
end

    -- Spawn a background thread for the HTTP request
    httpThread = love.thread.newThread([[
        local http    = require("socket.http")
        local ltn12   = require("ltn12")
        local channel = ...

        local body  = {}
        local ok, code = http.request({
            url    = "https://httpbin.org/get?game=love",
            sink   = ltn12.sink.table(body),
        })

title: Lobby / Room Pattern
description: Server-side lobby where players join, pick a name, and the host starts the game when ready.
code:
-- SERVER
local enet   = require("enet")
local host
local lobby  = {}     -- peer -> { name, ready }
local gameStarted = false
local MAX_PLAYERS = 4

local function broadcast(msg, except)
    for peer, _ in pairs(lobby) do
        if peer ~= except then peer:send(msg) end
    end
end

local function lobbyList()
    local names = {}
    for _, info in pairs(lobby) do table.insert(names, info.name) end
    return "LOBBY:" .. table.concat(names, ",")
end

function love.load()
    host = enet.host_create("*:6789", MAX_PLAYERS)
    print("Lobby open on :6789")
end

function love.update(dt)
    local event = host:service(0)
    while event do
        if event.type == "connect" then
            if gameStarted or host:peer_count() > MAX_PLAYERS then
                event.peer:send("REJECT:game_in_progress")
                event.peer:disconnect_later()
            else
                lobby[event.peer] = { name="Player"..host:peer_count(), ready=false }
                event.peer:send("WELCOME:" .. lobby[event.peer].name)
                broadcast(lobbyList())
            end

        elseif event.type == "receive" then
            local info = lobby[event.peer]
            if info then
                if event.data:match("^NAME:") then
                    info.name  = event.data:sub(6, 20)
                    broadcast(lobbyList())

                elseif event.data == "READY" then
                    info.ready = true
                    broadcast("READY:" .. info.name)
                    -- Start when all players ready
                    local allReady = true
                    for _, p in pairs(lobby) do if not p.ready then allReady=false end end
                    if allReady and host:peer_count() >= 2 then
                        broadcast("START")
                        gameStarted = true
                    end

                elseif event.data == "UNREADY" then
                    info.ready = false
                end
            end

        elseif event.type == "disconnect" then
            if lobby[event.peer] then
                local name = lobby[event.peer].name
                lobby[event.peer] = nil
                broadcast("LEFT:" .. name)
                broadcast(lobbyList())
            end
        end
        event = host:service(0)
    end
end

-- CLIENT messages to handle:
-- "WELCOME:<name>"  - assigned name
-- "LOBBY:<n1,n2>"  - current player list
-- "READY:<name>"   - player marked ready
-- "LEFT:<name>"    - player disconnected
-- "START"          - game begins, switch state
-- "REJECT:<reason>"- couldn't join

-- json.lua  (minimal, handles strings, numbers, booleans, arrays, objects)
local json = {}

function json.encode(val)
    local t = type(val)
    if val == nil    then return "null"
    elseif t == "boolean" then return tostring(val)
    elseif t == "number"  then return tostring(val)
    elseif t == "string"  then
        return '"' .. val:gsub('\\','\\\\'):gsub('"','\\"')
                          :gsub('\n','\\n'):gsub('\r','\\r') .. '"'
    elseif t == "table" then
        -- Array?
        local isArr = #val > 0
        local parts = {}
        if isArr then
            for _, v in ipairs(val) do table.insert(parts, json.encode(v)) end
            return "[" .. table.concat(parts, ",") .. "]"
        else
            for k, v in pairs(val) do
                table.insert(parts, json.encode(tostring(k)) .. ":" .. json.encode(v))
            end
            return "{" .. table.concat(parts, ",") .. "}"
        end
    end
    return "null"
end

title: Global Signal Bus
description: App-wide event bus - any module can emit or subscribe without direct references.
code:
-- Signal.lua  (global singleton)
local Signal = {}
local _handlers = {}

function Signal.on(name, fn)
    if not _handlers[name] then _handlers[name] = {} end
    table.insert(_handlers[name], fn)
    -- Return unsubscribe handle
    return function()
        local list = _handlers[name]
        for i = #list, 1, -1 do
            if list[i] == fn then table.remove(list, i) end
        end
    end
end

function Signal.once(name, fn)
    local unsub
    unsub = Signal.on(name, function(...)
        unsub()
        fn(...)
    end)
    return unsub
end

function Signal.emit(name, ...)
    for _, fn in ipairs(_handlers[name] or {}) do
        fn(...)
    end
end

function Signal.clear(name)
    _handlers[name] = nil
end

return Signal
